title: Defining DTD Mappings
breadcrumbs: Mapping Between Uniface and XML Streams
text: Defining DTD Mappings Define DTD mapping lists when you need to pass information via XML streams. You can define default mappings for modeled objects and local mappings for specific components or operations. If you are mapping data to Uniface entities with overlapping field names, the order items in the mapping list can be significant. The mapping list must follow the order in which elements first appear in the XML stream. For example, if you are mapping an XML stream to the entities COUNTRY and PERSON, and both these entities have a field NAME, then the order of the mapping list is significant. If COUNTRY data appears first in the XML stream, then your mapping list should specify a mapping for NAME.COUNTRY before specifying a mapping for NAME.PERSON. Note: Mappings are not saved to the DTD definition. If you make a change to the mapping, such as a name change, you need to ensure that the change is also made in the DTD. To define a default DTD mapping: Open the DTD in the DTD Editor and click Mapping. This opens the DTD Default Mapping form. For each element you want to map to a Uniface field or entity, Enter the name of the element in the DTD Item Name column and the name of the target field or entity in the Uniface Name column. The name of an element in the DTD is case-sensitive. It is good practice to use qualified names, such as Field Entity , and Entity Model If you qualify entity names with model names, then you need to specify a local mapping if the XML stream is also mapped to an identical entity in a different application model. For example, if the default mapping specifies that element country is mapped to the entity COUNTRY.ORD, you need to specify a local mapping to map the data to the entity COUNTRY.ORD_P. Click OK to accept the mapping definitions, or Cancel to discard the changes. Choose File >Save to store the changes in your Repository. Note: Whenever you rename an element in the DTD Wizard , a corresponding entry in the default mapping is created or updated. To define a local mapping: Define a local or component variable to contain the mapping information. Define a Uniface associative list with the following syntax: " ElementName=TargetName ;..." ElementName —name of an element in the DTD. ElementName is case-sensitive. TargetName —name of a field or entity. The fields or entities do not have to be present on the target component. Values that cannot be mapped to a painted field are ignored. TargetName is not case-sensitive. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
